name: Artifacts
desc: When receiving an artifact shipment, you will get a single artifact. The more points you have accumulated, the higher the chance of receiving a rarer and more powerful artifact
explanation: Receive a shipment of random artifacts. These artifacts provide a variety of bonuses and permanent boosts.
capacity: 1k * Planets Occupied stat (which may not match your actual owned planets)

name: Influence
desc: Influence can be increased by building influence structures on eligible planets, and increasing planetary availability with Charters.
explanation: Influence does not appear naturally on planets. Further, it requires special planning to build planets with significant Influence. Spend it on Zones, convening the Council, Voting, and other actions.
capacity: 22 * Planets Occupied stat (which may not match your actual owned planets)

name: Minerals
desc: When receiving a shipment of minerals, the current number of units will be converted into actual minerals and put directly in your cargo
explanation: Receive a shipment of Minerals. Structures and Modules can require these Minerals for construction, or you can sell these minerals for Credits.
capacity: 500 * Planets Occupied stat (which may not match your actual owned planets)

name: Research
desc: Research can be increased by hiring more scientists, building research structures on your planets, or redeeming Galaxy Points.
explanation: Spend Research on research topics to unlock various items. Some missions also require Research, and eventually you can sell Research in Research Contracts.

name: Research Studies
desc:
Generated hourly during planetary research production.

Used for advanced missions and Legion Ascendancy (coming soon). Each planet that produces research has a chance of generating a study each hour. Higher production increases the chance. More research planets = more chances to generate studies.
explanation: Research studies are granted by each planet individually, based on that planet's Research production. Your overall player production isn't relevant, just the production on each planet. Moreover, the chance for a planet to produce a Study isn't linear: increasing the Research on a planet gives diminishing returns. Thus, creating pure-Research planets isn't particularly effective. The exact calculation seems to be along the lines of: log(Research * 2.8 / 1000) / (10 * log(2.8)). Short version: spread out your Optimizers and make sure to construct structures that have a side of Research. A planet producing around 1k Research (maybe 1.1k, hard to ascertain for certain) will have a .1 chance of producing a study; bump this to 2.8k for a .2 chance, and about 8k for a .3 chance.
capacity: Based on Rank. Higher Rank gives more capacity, but with diminishing returns. Specifically, 49.912 * Rank ^ .4632. This is close to 50 times the square root of your rank.
